commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add isStruct convenience parameter to ProvidedTypeDefinition (#464)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Adds an optional `?isStruct: bool` parameter to both public constructors
of `ProvidedTypeDefinition`. This is a **Task 10 improvement** that
makes generating struct value types more discoverable and convenient.

## Motivation

Creating a generative struct type provider currently requires knowing to
set `baseType = Some typeof(System.ValueType)` explicitly:

```fsharp
// Before: easy to forget the right base type
let t = ProvidedTypeDefinition("Point", Some typeof(System.ValueType), isErased = false)
```

With this change, users can use a named parameter that mirrors the
existing `isInterface`, `isAbstract`, `isSealed` pattern:

````fsharp
// After: explicit and self-documenting
let t = ProvidedTypeDefinition("Point", None, isStruct = true, isErased = false)
```

The traditional approach still works unchanged (full backward
compatibility).

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/ProvidedTypes.fs` | Add `isStruct` to `defaultAttributes`; add
`?isStruct: bool` to both public constructors |
| `src/ProvidedTypes.fsi` | Update constructor signatures to expose
`?isStruct: bool` |
| `tests/GenerativeStructProvisionTests.fs` | 4 new tests:
`IsValueType`, `IsSealed`, `SequentialLayout`, traditional-approach |
| `tests/FSharp.TypeProviders.SDK.Tests.fsproj` | Include new test file
|

## Behaviour when `isStruct = true`

1. `TypeAttributes.SequentialLayout` is added to the type attributes
(matches what the F# and C# compilers emit for struct types by default)
2. The base type is set to `Some typeof(System.ValueType)` when `None`
is passed — this is what drives `IsValueType` detection, the IL type
classification, and the assembly writer's `SetParent` call

## Test Status

All 108 tests pass (104 existing + 4 new):

```
Passed!  - Failed: 0, Passed: 108, Skipped: 0, Total: 108, Duration: 5 s
````

fix(terrain): 513x513 overlap tiles to eliminate chunk seam gaps

Root cause of dark lines at chunk boundaries:
- Tile A UV u=1.0 → source pixel x*512+511
- Tile B UV u=0.0 → source pixel (x+1)*512+0 = x*512+512
- 1 pixel gap → height discontinuity → visible seam

Fix: 513×513 tiles where pixel 512 = first pixel of next tile.
Now Tile A at u=1.0 and Tile B at u=0.0 sample the same source pixel.

- tiling.js: 513×513 tiles with overlap (TILE_SIZE+1 stride)
- DataTexture: 513×513 (TW = tileSize+1)
- HeightmapDecoder: count = (tileSize+1)^2
- ChunkWorkerPool: slot = (tileSize+1)^2
- WorldManager.getHeightAt: stride TW=513, range 0..T (512)
- WorldManager._buildSkirt: stride TW=513
- terrain.vert.glsl: texel 1/513

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove activity watchdog that kills working agents

The activity watchdog was terminating Copilot SDK sessions that appeared
idle but were actually working (waiting for tool responses, running long
builds, or processing large codebases). This caused items to fail with
'Activity timeout: no output for Xs' even when the agent was actively
working.

Changes:
- Remove session_watchdog.py (activity watchdog implementation)
- Remove watchdog setup/cancel logic from copilot_sdk.py
- Remove activity_timeout handling from sdk_helpers._check_early_exit
- Remove watchdog_abort from sdk_helpers._await_completion
- Remove ActivityWatchdogConfig from config.py
- Add idle_timeout_seconds as a top-level ProjectConfig field (migrated
  from the removed ActivityWatchdogConfig)
- Add backward-compat migration: config files with activity_watchdog key
  will have idle_timeout_seconds extracted before strict parsing
- Remove all watchdog tests from test files

The existing per-item timeout (max_timeout / 7200s default) is sufficient
protection against truly stuck sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(terrain): clean 512x512 tiles + flipY=true UV fix

Root cause of all remaining issues: rotateX(-PI/2) flips UV V-axis,
UV v=0 -> world Z=oz+W but DataTexture row 0 was oz -> Z-mirrored terrain.

Changes:
- tiling.js: clean 512x512 tiles (no overlap pixel, simpler code)
- DataTexture: flipY=true so row 0 (oz) lands at UV v=1 correctly
- HeightmapDecoder.worker: count = tileSize^2 (512x512)
- ChunkWorkerPool: slot size back to tileSize^2
- WorldManager.getHeightAt: stride=512, bilinear on T-1 range
- WorldManager._buildSkirt: full rewrite, clean 512 stride,
  row 0=oz top, row T-1=oz+W bottom, STEP sampling, BOT=-300
- terrain.vert.glsl: texel 1/513 -> 1/512

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(e2e): use cookie-based auth injection + fix super-admin landing redirect

  Next.js middleware (which reads cookies, not localStorage) passes auth checks.
  Also sets localStorage for client-side SDK reads via a temp page on the right origin.
- 01-navigation.spec.ts: injectAuth rewritten to use context.addCookies() only
  (cookies alone are sufficient since middleware reads them; localStorage on wrong
  origin was ineffective anyway).
- auth/01-login.spec.ts: stale-token test now uses context.addCookies() then
  context.clearCookies() to properly simulate stale session.
- auth/02-logout.spec.ts: stale-session test now also calls context.clearCookies()
  since middleware ignores localStorage.
- lims/03-invalid-transition-blocked.spec.ts: inline auth injection updated to
  use context.addCookies() instead of localStorage-only evaluation.
- tests/10-invalid-transition-blocked.spec.ts: same cookie injection fix.
- admin/src/lib/admin-access.ts: resolveAdminLanding() now short-circuits to
  '/dashboard' for isSuperAdmin users, bypassing the permissions loop that was
  causing super-admin users to land on /account instead of /dashboard.

commit.message:
refactor(devreload): extract inline JS to embedded resource

Move ~170 lines of inline JavaScript from DevReloadMiddleware.fs to
Resources/devreload.js as an embedded resource. The JS template uses
mustache-style placeholders ({{SSE_URL}}, {{RELOAD_GUARD_THRESHOLD}},
etc.) that are replaced at runtime from DevReloadConfig.defaults.

Benefits:
- JS is now lintable/formattable independently
- No more sprintf %% escaping for CSS percent signs
- Config values flow from the single DevReloadConfig source of truth
- DevReloadMiddleware.fs drops from ~320 to ~150 lines

Adds 8 tests verifying embedded resource loading, placeholder
templating, and script structure.

commit.message:
`AddAIContentType` automatically registers the content type against every base in the inheritance chain up to `AIContent` (#7358)

* Initial plan

* Make AddAIContentType with baseType parameter public

Add two new public overloads of AddAIContentType that accept a baseType
parameter, allowing consumers to register AIContent-derived types in
custom polymorphic hierarchies (e.g., under ToolCallContent or
ToolResultContent, not just AIContent).

- Generic: AddAIContentType<TContent>(options, baseType, discriminatorId)
- Non-generic: AddAIContentType(options, baseType, contentType, discriminatorId)

Updates API baseline and adds comprehensive unit tests.

Co-authored-by: jozkee <16040868+jozkee@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-register AddAIContentType across full inheritance chain

Instead of adding new baseType overloads, modify the existing
AddAIContentType methods to automatically walk the inheritance
chain from the content type up to AIContent, registering against
every intermediate base type. This eliminates the need for callers
to manually register against each level in the hierarchy.

The internal registration of experimental types (CodeInterpreter,
ImageGeneration) is simplified from 8 calls to 4, since the chain
walker handles intermediate bases (ToolCallContent/ToolResultContent)
automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add CHANGELOG entry for AddAIContentType chain registration
